title: Room 1: The Locked Study
description: A wall safe hums behind a framed quote. Dusty books hide three symbols etched into the desk.
question: What keyword in programming usually means 'if this is true, do the next thing'?
hint: Think about code deciding between paths.
success: The safe clicks open and a brass key slides out.

title: Room 2: The Server Hall
description: Rows of monitors flicker to life. One terminal demands that you identify the user-facing layer.
question: Which option best describes the front end of a web app?
hint: It is the layer that shapes the user's experience.
success: The monitors turn green and a hidden door unlocks.

title: Room 3: The Archive Vault
description: Steel drawers line the walls. A note says the right answer keeps information organized and reusable.
question: What structure is commonly used to store related values under named keys?
hint: Think of key-value pairs.
success: A drawer slides open and reveals the next access card.

title: Room 1: Scene Workshop
description: Blueprints cover the table. A projector asks how reusable screens are saved in Godot.
question: What is the reusable building block you save and instance in Godot?
hint: You can open it as a `.tscn` file.
success: The projector flickers green and the workshop door unlocks.

title: Room 2: Node Gallery
description: Rows of floating panels list node names. One panel asks which node is meant for clickable UI.
question: Which Godot node is commonly used for a clickable UI control?
hint: It emits a pressed signal.
success: A panel slides aside and reveals the next hallway.

title: Room 3: Signal Terminal
description: A terminal pulses with connection lines between nodes.
question: What feature lets one Godot node notify another when something happens?
hint: Buttons and timers use this a lot.
success: The terminal accepts your answer and opens the exit gate.

title: Room 1: Algebra Vault
description: A chalkboard glows with symbols. The lock opens only if you solve for the unknown.
question: If 2x + 6 = 14, what is x?
hint: Subtract 6 first, then divide by 2.
success: The chalkboard clears and the first gate slides open.

title: Room 2: Fraction Bridge
description: A bridge appears in pieces. A panel asks you to simplify the fraction to complete it.
question: What is 12/16 simplified?
hint: Divide numerator and denominator by 4.
success: The missing bridge section locks into place.

title: Room 3: Graph Chamber
description: A moving graph projects on the wall. The door asks for the slope of a simple line.
question: What is the slope between points (1, 2) and (3, 6)?
hint: Use rise over run.
success: The graph stabilizes and reveals the exit.

title: Room 1: Grammar Library
description: Stacks of books whisper clues. A sentence waits for the correct part of speech.
question: In the sentence 'She quickly ran home,' what part of speech is 'quickly'?
hint: It describes how she ran.
success: The books hush, and the shelf-door opens.

title: Room 2: Synonym Hall
description: Portraits line the hall. One asks for a word closest in meaning to 'brave.'
question: Which word is the best synonym for 'brave'?
hint: Think fearless.
success: The portraits nod and the next passage unlocks.

title: Room 3: Reading Chamber
description: A paragraph glows on the wall. The room asks what the author's main point is called.
question: What do we call the central point of a paragraph or passage?
hint: It is the big idea the text supports.
success: The glowing text fades into an open doorway.

title: Room 1: Algorithm Forge
description: Mechanical arms sort glowing cubes. The system asks what an algorithm is.
question: Which option best defines an algorithm?
hint: Think method, not machine.
success: The forge hums smoothly and the first gate opens.

title: Room 2: Data Tower
description: Holographic arrays float in the air. A console asks which structure follows FIFO order.
question: Which data structure follows first-in, first-out order?
hint: Think of a line of people waiting.
success: The tower rotates and reveals the next staircase.

title: Room 3: Binary Lock
description: A keypad flashes in two symbols only. The lock asks how many values a binary digit can hold.
question: How many possible values can a single binary digit represent?
hint: Think 0 and 1.
success: The binary lights settle into a safe green pattern.

title: Room 1: Whiteboard Arena
description: A whiteboard lights up with a coding prompt. The panel asks about time complexity notation.
question: What is Big O notation used to describe?
hint: It measures efficiency trends, not exact seconds.
success: The whiteboard clears and the next interview room unlocks.

title: Room 2: Debug Chamber
description: Error logs float around the room. One screen asks what you should do first when debugging.
question: What is usually the best first debugging step?
hint: You need a reliable way to observe the bug.
success: The logs fade away and the chamber door unlocks.

title: Room 3: System Design Gate
description: A giant diagram fills the wall. A voice asks what helps a service handle more traffic.
question: Which approach helps a service handle more users by adding more machines?
hint: Think scaling out, not just scaling up.
success: The system diagram reconfigures and the gate opens.

title: Room 1: Lab Entry
description: Beakers bubble under blue light. A panel asks which state of matter keeps shape and volume.
question: Which state of matter has a definite shape and a definite volume?
hint: Its particles are packed closely together.
success: The beakers settle and the lab door clicks open.

title: Room 2: Energy Core
description: A dim reactor asks what plants use to turn sunlight into food.
question: What process lets plants convert sunlight into energy?
hint: It uses light, water, and carbon dioxide.
success: The reactor brightens and powers the next room.

title: Room 3: Orbit Hall
description: Planets spin overhead. The final lock asks which force keeps planets in orbit around the sun.
question: What force keeps planets moving around the sun?
hint: It pulls masses toward one another.
success: The orbit projection aligns and the hall opens.

title: Room 1: Ancient Archive
description: Stone tablets line the walls. A voice asks which river valley civilization built pyramids.
question: Which ancient civilization is famous for building pyramids?
hint: Think Nile River.
success: A stone panel rises and reveals the path forward.

title: Room 2: Revolution Chamber
description: Flags sway in a hidden breeze. A plaque asks what event started in 1775 in America.
question: What major conflict began in 1775 between the American colonies and Britain?
hint: It led to American independence.
success: The plaque glows and unlocks the next archive.

title: Room 3: World History Gate
description: A world map lights up with trade routes. The gate asks what connected Europe and Asia for trade.
question: What network of trade routes connected Europe and Asia?
hint: Silk, spices, and ideas traveled along it.
success: Trade routes illuminate the final exit.
